summary: Internet Computer app with industry-standard RBAC. First registered user becomes admin, principal-based authentication, admins can create/manage users with role-based access control (RBAC). Fixing authorization error when admin tries to add users.
architectural_notes: First registered principal is automatically assigned admin role and stored in canister state
Admin authorization uses principal-based checks (no hardcoded principals)
Admins can grant/revoke roles for other users
known_issues: Admin receives 'Unauthorized: Only admins can create users' error when attempting to add users

existing_features.title: Update all frontend API calls in SettingsPage.tsx, CreateUserDialog.tsx, EditUserDialog.tsx, and any related hooks in useQueries.ts to pass the authenticated Internet Identity principal through the actor when invoking user management backend functions. Ensure the actor used for these calls is the authenticated actor (not anonymous) so the backend receives the correct caller principal.

Implement a backend function that allows the current admin to grant admin role to other registered principals, and another to revoke admin role. Expose these in the frontend SettingsPage under user management so admins can promote or demote users.

Add a backend query function that returns the current caller's role and admin status based on their Internet Identity principal. Use this in the frontend AuthContext or Header to correctly display the admin role badge and control access to admin-only UI sections without relying solely on locally stored session data.

new_feature_requests.summary: RBAC: First registered user becomes admin, principal-based role checks, admin can create/manage users

Implement industry-standard RBAC in the backend: the first principal to register is automatically assigned the admin role and stored in stable state. All subsequent admin authorization checks must compare the caller's Internet Identity principal against the stored admin principal list. Remove or replace any hardcoded principal or password-based admin checks that currently cause the 'Unauthorized: Only admins can create users' error.
